description: Native English. Female, early 30s. High quality. Persona: Field ecologist, self-exiled heiress. Emotion: Composed, dry, quietly warm. Low, even alto pitched to carry across open ground without ever rising; a placeless, privately educated, faintly transatlantic accent she has deliberately roughened. Economical and measured, short declarative sentences that land on one flat, unmistakable verdict.
text: [serious] Watch the water a season. The soil. What comes back. Build it so it can keep itself... so it stands on its own, needs no one's permission. [deliberate] An ecosystem has no unnecessary species. People are no different.
